name: Monitoring modality mix optimization
consequences: Adjusting the balance between low-burden nightly monitoring and high-fidelity PSG nights changes data richness, participant burden, and operational costs. A higher share of full PSG nights improves event capture and sleep staging accuracy but raises staffing demands and participant strain; this trade-off can constrain enrollment pace if safety thresholds are not met.
options: Expand enhanced nights to all participants on a rotating schedule to maximize data richness.
Limit enhanced nights to cases with flagged events and rely on dry-electrode and mattress sensors for screening.
Introduce automated real-time alarm-based escalation nights triggered by sensor anomalies to balance data yield and safety.
review: Controls safety and ethical oversight vs data throughput and study pace. Weakness: The options fail to consider participant burden and regulatory feasibility.

name: Recruitment strategy flexibility
consequences: Expanding eligibility and recruitment channels increases the eligible pool and can accelerate enrollment, but may raise sample heterogeneity and screening workload. Broader criteria can dilute effect detection but improve generalizability; expanded channels require standardized pre-admission processes to maintain comparability.
options: Broaden eligibility to 18–70 with a standardized safety pre-screen to maintain risk control.
Scale recruitment through regional clinics with uniform pre-admission screening to reduce site heterogeneity.
Implement retention-focused incentives and enhanced participant communication to improve stay completion.
review: Controls enrollment pace vs sample heterogeneity. Weakness: The options fail to consider retention challenges and comparability to planned control group.

name: Data governance and external collaboration strategy
consequences: Relaxed data-sharing policies can enhance external validation and collaboration but require stronger privacy safeguards, consent management, and governance. Stricter controls protect participant privacy but may limit external replication and cross-site validation; governance must balance openness with risk control.
options: Deposit de-identified datasets into public repositories with controlled access.
Restrict sharing to internal university partners with tiered access and formal data-use agreements.
Create a data-use governance board to review external collaboration requests and privacy safeguards.
review: Controls openness of data sharing vs privacy risk management. Weakness: The options fail to consider consent scope across institutions.

name: Staffing and scorer workflow optimization
consequences: Increasing double scoring and cross-training improves reliability and throughput but raises labor costs and potential fatigue. A more versatile workforce can accelerate data processing but requires robust training and supervision to maintain quality.
options: Cross-train technicians to perform collection and initial scoring, expanding versatile staffing.
Institute double-scoring with independent raters and an adjudication round for all events.
Adopt a rotating night-shift model with on-call senior scorer for complex cases.
review: Controls throughput of scoring vs inter-rater reliability. Weakness: The options fail to consider budgetary constraints and fatigue.

name: Event-triage tool development and validation
consequences: Semi-automation can reduce manual review and speed adjudication, but introduces bias risk and requires rigorous validation and ongoing oversight. Early pilot results must be designed to preserve interpretability and human verification to avoid misclassification.
options: Develop and validate a semi-automated event-triage model using retrospective labeled data before live use.
Adopt a transparent rule-based triage approach with predefined criteria and human-in-the-loop verification.
Pilot cross-stream validation comparing tool outputs against dual human scoring across participants.
review: Controls automation of triage vs human oversight. Weakness: The options fail to consider bias and validation requirements.

name: Facility safety and naturalistic environment balance
consequences: Modular room design and flexible safety features preserve ecological validity while ensuring participant safety, but may constrain layout and workflow optimization. Safer environments can limit certain observational dynamics without harming data quality if designed thoughtfully.
options: Implement modular room configurations that can adapt between domestic-like and safety-focused layouts.
Enhance real-time monitoring and emergency response with redundant alarms and cross-trained staff.
Minimize camera footprint to zones with consent while enabling safety-focused video access for flagged events.
review: Controls ecological validity vs safety overhead. Weakness: The options fail to consider regulatory approvals for environment changes.

name: PSG escalation nights protocol formalization
consequences: Formalizing escalation nights increases the likelihood of capturing high-resolution PSG data during flagged periods, supporting robust event characterization. Downstream, this will require tighter scheduling, higher staffing during escalation windows, and clearer criteria for when escalation is triggered, potentially increasing nightly workload during escalation weeks.
options: Institute a structured escalation night protocol featuring full PSG on nights flagged by automated detectors.
Implement a rotating escalation schedule to align high-detail data capture with participant milestones and clinic workload.
Reserve escalation nights for nights with clear event indicators to maximize data yield while limiting routine intrusion.
review: Controls high-resolution PSG escalation nights vs routine nocturnal monitoring. Weakness: The options fail to consider escalation costs and staff workload implications.

name: Residential experience optimization under safety-by-design
consequences: Enhancing room ambiance and safety features supports participant comfort and longer residency, which increases data capture opportunities. Downstream, comfort-driven adjustments may introduce variability in environmental conditions that complicate cross-participant standardization and statistical comparison.
options: Enhance room ambiance and privacy controls to improve participant comfort while maintaining monitoring fidelity.
Adopt a flexible scheduling and meal plan to boost retention without compromising data collection.
Integrate modular environmental adjustments (lighting, sound) that preserve naturalistic sleep but require extra safety monitoring.
review: Controls participant comfort vs data fidelity. Weakness: The options fail to consider impact on data consistency and incident reporting overhead.

name: EEG-BIDS data governance and local storage hardening
consequences: Strengthening the data pipeline with standardized metadata, encryption, and robust local backups reduces risk of data loss and misannotation. Downstream, these controls may slow ad-hoc data exports and require ongoing staff training to maintain compliance.
options: Enforce automated metadata tagging and local encryption to reduce data handling errors.
Establish a local NAS redundancy plan with offline backups to mitigate data loss.
Standardize data de-identification pipelines before export to repositories.
review: Controls local storage resilience vs rapid data sharing. Weakness: The options fail to consider cross-site data harmonization timelines.

name: Dual-scorer calibration and scoring efficiency
consequences: Regular calibration sustains high inter-rater reliability and reduces bottlenecks in data review, supporting timely analyses. Downstream, calibration demand and clearer guidelines increase training needs and may slow early data turnover.
options: Schedule regular inter-rater calibration sessions to maintain scoring reliability.
Implement a blinded adjudication workflow with independent raters for all nights.
Pilot a semi-automated scoring assistant to support raters without replacing human judgment.
review: Controls inter-rater reliability vs. review throughput. Weakness: The options fail to consider training resource demands and annotation backlog.

name: Artifact management and sensor reliability enhancement
consequences: Improved sensor reliability reduces data gaps and improves overall data integrity, enabling more robust phenotyping. Downstream, maintenance activity and downtime may increase staffing demands and require buffer capacity.
options: Institute routine sensor calibration and redundancy checks for night shifts.
Deploy protocol for rapid replacement of faulty sensors to minimize data gaps.
Adopt a lightweight participant-logged artifact flag process to aid review.
review: Controls data quality vs operational disruption. Weakness: The options fail to consider replacement lead times and supply chain constraints.

name: Informed consent optimization and collateral history integration
consequences: Integrating collateral history into the consent process improves eligibility screening and participant understanding, potentially increasing enrollment quality. Downstream, broader consent scopes may extend pre-admission timelines and require more extensive governance oversight.
options: Integrate structured collateral history gathering into the consent workflow to improve eligibility screening.
Offer tiered consent options for different data uses to align with participant comfort.
Provide a pre-admission educational session to reduce dropouts during enrollment.
review: Controls consent breadth vs enrollment stability. Weakness: The options fail to consider regulatory review timelines and ethics gatekeeping.

name: Sensing modality hierarchy reconfiguration
consequences: Adopting a tiered sensing scheme shifts emphasis toward comfortable, low-burden streams on routine nights, which can increase participant retention and data yield from naturalistic nights. A downstream implication is the need for robust alerting and a clear escalation protocol to avoid missed events during early nights when high-resolution data are absent.
options: Default to dry-electrode EEG headbands and mattress sensors for routine nights to optimize comfort and data yield.
Activate a rapid escalation to full PSG on any suspected parasomnia cue to maximize diagnostic precision.
Pilot adaptive weighting across streams based on prior data quality to balance data richness with participant burden.
review: Controls data fidelity vs. participant burden. Weakness: The options fail to consider escalation timing and potential gaps in event capture during early nights.

name: Staffing model for night coverage and incident response
consequences: Reconfiguring staffing affects incident response speed and safety during overnight observations and admission peaks; it can reduce fatigue if planned well but may raise payroll costs and training needs, potentially slowing expansion.
options: Maintain a fixed multi-shift coverage plan to ensure rapid incident response across all eight suites.
Implement on-call escalation for flagged events to reduce overnight staffing costs.
Cross-train technicians to perform multiple roles and share load during peak weeks.
review: Controls safety of overnight operations vs. cost efficiency. Weakness: The options fail to consider staff burnout and long-term retention.

name: Privacy-preserving data sharing and de-identification strategy
consequences: Enables compliant data sharing while protecting participant confidentiality; may reduce granularity and limit certain analyses, requiring clear governance and robust de-identification, which can increase processing time and coordination.
options: De-identify datasets with exhaustive removal of direct identifiers before any external sharing.
Publish aggregated metrics with controlled access to raw signals under governance.
Enforce tiered data-sharing agreements that grant clinicians access within the university network.
review: Controls privacy protection vs. data utility. Weakness: The options fail to consider the risks of re-identification through multi-modal streams.

name: Recruitment funnel optimization under safety constraints
consequences: Streamlines candidate intake and increases enrollment efficiency without compromising safety; however, stricter exclusions may reduce the eligible pool and slow recruitment, affecting project timeline and statistical power.
options: Expand recruitment to regional sleep clinics to widen the eligible pool.
Tighten pre-screening with collateral histories to reduce in-lab dropouts.
Offer modular participation with non-overnight components to increase enrollment reach.
review: Controls recruitment pace vs. safety risk. Weakness: The options fail to consider long-term participant diversity and retention across the cohort.

name: Data quality assurance workflow for multi-stream annotation
consequences: Strengthens inter-rater reliability and data integrity across streams, enabling robust longitudinal phenotyping; but it increases manual review load and staff time, potentially slowing turn-around on event adjudication.
options: Implement double-scoring on all flagged events with adjudication by a senior scorer.
Introduce semi-automated flagging to reduce manual review load while preserving accuracy.
Schedule regular calibration sessions and ongoing quality audits to align scorers.
review: Controls scoring accuracy vs. resource use. Weakness: The options fail to consider potential biases in scorer training and drift over time.

name: Phased expansion threshold for suite capacity
consequences: Guides scalable growth aligned to data quality and safety outcomes; delays expansion may slow accrual and discoveries, while premature scaling can strain safety, staffing, and regulatory compliance.
options: Proceed to add more suites once data quality metrics meet predefined benchmarks.
Delay expansion to prioritize data maturation and staffing efficiency.
Implement a modular expansion where additional suites are rented temporarily during peak enrollment.
review: Controls expansion timing vs. capital risk. Weakness: The options fail to consider long-term maintenance of the facility and ongoing safety training as capacity grows.
